channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More evacuations are ordered as another wildfire breaks out in the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More evacuations were underway across Los Angeles on Thursday as another fire broke out. The Los Angeles Fire Department issued orders Thursday afternoon for what’s known as the Kenneth Fire.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump waits on Supreme Court after New York's high court declines to stop his hush money sentencing
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York's highest court on Thursday declined to block Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case, leaving the U.S. Supreme Court as the president-elect's likely last option to prevent it from taking place as scheduled on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, MICHAEL R. SISAK and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter was celebrated Thursday for his personal humility and public service before, during and after his presidency during a funeral at Washington National Cathedral featuring the kind of pageantry the 39th U.S. president typically eschewed.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Democrats join Republicans in voting to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats voted with Republicans on Thursday to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — signaling that they will try and find spots to work with President-elect Don
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Mexico is a go-to state for women seeking abortions. A new court ruling helps it stay that way
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The New Mexico Supreme Court on Thursday struck down abortion restrictions by conservative cities and counties, helping to ensure the state remains a go-to destination for people from other states with bans.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aerial video shows before and after views of Palisades Fire in Los Angeles
item.description: “I couldn’t image what we were preparing to see once we got over here on this side of the hill,” pilot Rich Prickett said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Photo gallery: Aerial photos show the scope of destruction from the Pacific Palisades wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Street after street, there is little but dark ash and ruin where a wealthy seaside enclave favored by celebrities once stood. Blackened trees stand out amid the rubble, and the occasional mansion remains, suddenly exposed to view from all sides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stars lose homes in Los Angeles area fires and Jamie Lee Curtis pledges $1M to relief effort
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires burning in and around Los Angeles have claimed the homes of numerous celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Jeff Bridges, Paris Hilton and R&B star Jhené Aiko, and led to sweeping disruptions of entertainment events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge scraps Biden's Title IX rules, reversing expansion of protections for LGBTQ+ students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration’s Title IX rules expanding protections for LGBTQ+ students have been struck down nationwide after a federal judge in Kentucky found they overstepped the president’s authority. In a decision issued Thursday, U.S. District Judge Danny C.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Obama doesn't attend state funeral for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama did not attend the state funeral Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah firefighters answer the call to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: After a quick briefing, they hit the road to begin a 12-hour drive to California to battle the deadly and devastating Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned that there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 180,000 residents under evacuation orders in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters battled early Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California fire victim dies holding hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eaton Fire victim dies holding a hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Monkey found after California traffic stop is getting a new home
item.description: "The driver had a baby monkey attached - or clenched - on his person. That's something you don't see every day.
item.dc:creator: Briana Byus, NewsNation

item.title: Biden eulogizes ‘great American’ Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Thursday eulogized former President Carter, hailing the 39th president as a man of character who left an enduring legacy both during his time in the White House and after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter memorial: Suspect accused in Capitol Hill security breach during Trump visit identified
item.description: Capitol police have identified the 35-year-old man accused of trying to light his spray-painted car on fire near where former President Carter was lying in state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: ICE looking to expand migrant detention facilities across country, ACLU says
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union says information it has received from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement indicates that several migrant detention facilities on the South Texas border are among areas where the agency are seeking information to possibly expand under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: How to safely evacuate during a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along. Aaron Samson positioned his 83-year-old father-in-law behind his blue walker, and they began shuffling down the sidewalk.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The plunging polar vortex brought subfreezing temperatures Tuesday to some of the southernmost points of the U.S., threatening to dump snow on parts of Texas and Oklahoma in coming days and contributing to a power outage in Virginia's capital that made the water unsafe to drink.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WGN 9

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden creating two national monuments in California
item.description: President Biden is creating two new national monuments in California, the White House announced Tuesday. Biden will officially designate the Chuckwalla National Monument and the Sáttítla Highlands National Monument — protecting a combined 848,000 acres of particular significance to tribes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9
